text:
Where the catheter is necessitated by a permanent or temporary loss of bladder control, 
skilled nursing services that are provided at a frequency appropriate to the type of 
catheter in use would be considered reasonable and necessary. Absent complications, 
Foley catheters generally require skilled care once approximately every 30 days and 
silicone catheters generally require skilled care once every 60-90 days and this frequency 
of service would be considered reasonable and necessary.
